medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Mr. ___ is a left-handed man with no reported past medical history who presented to ___ with dizziness, diplopia and vertigo beginning this morning at 6 AM. He reported a headache on one side, nausea, vomiting, and difficulty walking in the morning with heavy legs. Visual acuity was noted to be abnormal bilaterally. A head CT showed hypodensity in the left cerebellum, leading to transfer for further evaluation.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital signs: T: 99.5, P: 60, R: 16, BP: 130/78, SaO2: 100% RA. Patient was awake, cooperative, and in no acute distress. Neurological examination showed alert mental status, able to follow commands, slight confusion with details.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: Difficulty with complex commands, left-right confusion, and vertical diplopia, alongside normal motor function. No sensory deficits. DTRs were normal. Mild dysmetria on left-sided coordination. Gait was normal with a narrow base.
